texto: That's what got her, of course. That everyone thought it so unbelievable that she could possibly attract a man like him. It shouldn't upset her because it was true. She couldn't. Not in this world, in this lifetime. Yet she didn't appreciated everyone else acting as if they were the most improbable twosome since Quasimodo hit on Esmeralda.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jo Leigh: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “That's what got her, of course. That everyone thought it so unbelievable that she could possibly …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, humor, looks pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de looks e romance.

texto: Gwen: It's not going to work.Paul: Pardon me?Gwen: Cinderella's not going to sleep with you because you're taking the ugly stepsister to the ball. She'll still make you wait.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jo Leigh que provoca temas ligados a amor. Ao questionar “Gwen: It's not going to work.Paul: Pardon me?Gwen: Cinderella's not going to sleep with you becau…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, cinderella, humor, com foco em trabalho e humor.
